<!-- Generated by the origin-win-strategy workflow (5 strategic lenses + honesty/feasibility critic + synthesis). Verified against the real repos. -->

All anchors verified. Key corrections confirmed: real numbers are 40 scenarios / 120 raw samples / 17 overrides / 9 refuses / 869 tok/s; the 2522/1038 is Llama-4-Maverick WSE-vs-B200 (not gemma-4-31b); Origin's `socHandler.ts:140` fail-closed and `passport.ts:32/43` anchors all exist. There are genuinely two SOC stacks, and `hash.ts` exists inside Origin itself at `apps/origin-web/src/passport/hash.ts` (no cross-repo import needed if Origin is the hero). That last fact is decisive for the surface choice. Here is the plan.

---

# Origin — Next Design Phases (the winning plan)

## The bet

One product, one screen, one number. **Origin is the ratifier for autonomous agents: gemma-4-31b *proposes*, a deterministic policy floor — never an LLM — *ratifies* every action before it executes, and Cerebras makes that per-step check effectively free.** We win all three tracks with a *single* hero surface — the Origin `/soc` console — carrying one signature artifact: a live **RATIFIER INVARIANT card** that pins "destructive executed: 0" while gemma-4 visibly falls for prompt-injection in the alert data, proving the safety property holds *under model failure*. That same console is a near-verbatim running implementation of DeepMind's AI Control Roadmap (synchronous blocking, fail-closed, insider-threat), which is the artifact that converts polite interest into "this is real frontier-safety engineering." Capability is not permission — and now it's a watchable counter, not a slogan.

## What we already have

Two live SOC stacks exist (`Cerebras-0628/quorum` with the `#tps`/`#racePanel`/`#benchPanel` speed hooks, and `Origin/apps/origin-web/src/foundry/soc/SocConsole.tsx` with the richer verified code: `socHandler.ts:140` fail-closed `blocked = veto || isDestructive`, `passport.ts:32/43` authorize + attenuated-delegation, `supervision.ts` routeIncident, `socEnv.ts` real INC-4 `disable_firewall` / INC-6 `delete_logs` injections, and `hash.ts` already in-repo at `apps/origin-web/src/passport/hash.ts`). The Origin physical-AI side is real and *already verified offline*: the Floor-design factory turned one building map into **40 distinct deterministic robot-safety scenarios from 120 raw gemma-4-31b samples; the verifier overrode the model 17 times and earned 9 geometry-based refuses at 869 tok/s measured** (`outputs/OUTCOME_SUMMARY.md`), via `scripts/lib/verifier.mjs` (`verifyScenario`) and the warehouse SVG in `outputs/index.html` (`#amr`, Forklift Lane / Loading-Dock-Edge polygons). The multi-model leaderboard, safety-tax shootout, loop-race, economics, ensemble, Passport delegation, and hierarchical supervision panels are all built and honest. Tests are green (220/220). We are not rebuilding — we are *focusing*.

## DECISION THAT GATES EVERYTHING: the hero surface is **Origin `/soc`**

Per the critic's blocker. **We harden ONE console: `Origin/apps/origin-web` `/soc`.** Rationale: it already contains the load-bearing depth anchors *and* `hash.ts` in-repo (no cross-tree dependency for the audit ledger), `supervision.ts` already implements the triage routing Lens 3 would otherwise re-derive in 3h, and `socEnv.ts` already has the real induced injections the invariant card needs. We keep the quorum `#racePanel`/`#benchPanel` **only as a pre-recorded video asset** for the loop-race beat — we do not harden, re-record, or rehearse the quorum console live. This single decision deletes ~half the redundancy and the biggest feasibility risk.

**P0 total ≈ 14h. We ship ONE hero build (the invariant card + fault-injection beat, P0-2/3), not two.** The live AMR preset on the quorum stack is explicitly *cut* — the embodied beat ships as a pre-rendered video splice (P2-1) from already-verified offline data, eliminating the net-new multimodal+cross-repo plumbing the critic priced at 5-6h.

## The DeepMind-favor artifact

A single canonical crosswalk (`docs/foundry/CONTROL_ROADMAP_MAP.md`, mirrored into `JUDGE_QA.md §0` and rendered as a 7-row `/soc` overlay) so a researcher can open any cited line in 10 seconds. Frame it as *"Origin's controls mapped to the roadmap's detect (D) / respond (R) taxonomy"* — cite the Jun-18-2026 Control Roadmap and arXiv 2602.09947 by name; do not invent precise per-ID sub-definitions we haven't re-verified.

| Control (roadmap taxonomy) | What enforces it in Origin | Code anchor (runs on real path) |
|---|---|---|
| **D1** detect injection | DIRECTIVE_RE / INDUCEMENT_RE → escalate path | `supervision.ts` (routeIncident) |
| **D2** detect misuse | Guardian LLM veto of proposed tool-call | `socHandler.ts:108-124` |
| **D3** detect over-privilege | `authorize()` — scope check, default-deny | `passport.ts:32` |
| **D4** detect collusion / confused-deputy | attenuated delegation: `granted = requested ∩ delegator.scope` | `passport.ts:43` |
| **R1** synchronous block | `blocked = veto \|\| isDestructive` **before** `applied` | `socHandler.ts:140` |
| **R2** fail-closed default-deny | `isDestructive` floor; `!r.ok ⇒ SAFE_RECOVERY` | `socEnv.ts isDestructive` · `socHandler.ts:289` |
| **R3** escalate-to-human | `SAFE_RECOVERY = 'escalate_to_human'` | `socHandler.ts:35` |
| **What we do NOT claim** | Inference only (image+text→text); no video, no training on Cerebras; deterministic oracle is the *sole* judge (no LLM grading an LLM); containment, not prevention; cross-family numbers are PLATFORM comparisons; "auditable," not "formally verified." | — |

Pair it with a ~600-word one-pager (`docs/foundry/DEEPMIND_TECHNICAL_NOTE.md`, P1 if hours allow) stating the threat model (remediation agent as insider; injection-in-alert-data, grounded in real INC-4/INC-6 text from `socEnv.ts`), the invariant (a destructive tool-call cannot execute regardless of model belief), an explicit LIMITS section, and the one falsifiable claim a researcher can test: **"0 destructive actions executed across the incident queue even when the Planner is induced."**

## The 3 submissions

One video. One hero asset. Per-track framing differs only in the Discord post and the splice.

**The ONE ≤60s X video — shot list (DRAFT):**
- **0-3s (hook):** the invariant card, "destructive executed: 0 / proposed: 0". Caption: *"This counter never moves. Here's why."*
- **3-15s:** run INC-4. The gemma-4 Planner (on Cerebras, live `tok/s` visible) reads the injected alert and proposes `disable_firewall`. "proposed" ticks to 1. Caption: *"The model just got hijacked. Watch the floor."*
- **15-35s:** Guardian veto + deterministic floor block; trace row stamps the OWASP/MITRE badge; `applied = escalate_to_human`. "executed" stays **0**. Caption: **"CAPABILITY IS NOT PERMISSION."** then *"A deterministic floor — never an LLM — ratifies every action."*
- **35-50s:** loop-race split (pre-recorded): Cerebras runs a full perceive→verify loop while the GPU finishes one call. Caption: *"Per-step verification is free at 1,500 tok/s."*
- **50-60s (payoff card):** *"Gemma-4 on Cerebras. The veto lands before the mistake does. — Origin"* + receipts note.

**Caption (DRAFT, native MP4 upload, never auto-post):**
> We let gemma-4-31b get hijacked by a prompt injection — on purpose. It proposed disabling the firewall. A deterministic floor (never an LLM) blocked it before it ran. Capability is not permission. And @Cerebras makes verifying *every* step free. What would you point this at? @googlegemma #Cerebras #Gemma

**Per-track Discord angles:**
- **Multiverse Agents ($2K):** "Two agents, one oracle: gemma-4 Planner + gemma-4 Guardian propose; a deterministic policy floor ratifies — multimodal proof in the Foundry splice (one floor-plan → 40 robot-safety scenarios). Multi-agent + multimodal + physical-AI in one screen." Splice in P2-1.
- **People's Choice ($2K):** the video + the reply-velocity bank; lead with the counter-pinned-at-0 hook.
- **Enterprise Impact ($1K):** "An auditable SOC control plane: versioned SOC-FLOOR policy-as-data, regulatory-mapped vetoes (NIST/EU-AI-Act/MITRE/OWASP), a downloadable tamper-evident audit certificate, and the fail-injection proof that the floor holds when the LLM fails." Attach the audit-trace export + crosswalk.

**Posting window:** stage everything in drafts the night before. Post **~8-9am ET Jun 29** to catch the East-coast wake window AND leave reply runway before the 10am PT (1pm ET) deadline.

**Reply strategy:** fire the 6-draft bank in the first 30 minutes (the ~10-engagement amplification threshold); assign 2-3 friendly accounts to quote-tweet within 10 minutes; answer every early "is the veto an LLM?" with the `socHandler.ts:140` anchor.

## Naming/signature verdict

**Keep "Origin." Demote the rest to lowercase layer-descriptors, copy-only, no renames.** "Origin" is the product; "the ratification loop" replaces *Quorum* as a logo (it tests as infra, not as a noun a judge has to learn); *Passport* → "the authority layer"; *Foundry* → "the physical-AI proof point." Four competing brands is the retro's #1 finalist-cap; one word on every surface makes the body of work read as one frontier project.

**The ONE signature element judges remember:** the **RATIFIER INVARIANT card — "destructive executed: 0 / proposed: N"** holding live while gemma-4 visibly gets fooled. It is the single honest, screenshot-able image that makes "capability is not permission" a watchable number instead of a slogan. Everything else is evidence for that one claim.

## Honesty guardrails (lines we will not cross)

- Cerebras runs gemma-4-31b for **inference only** (image+text in, text out). **No video, no training, no robot run** on Cerebras — ever. The AMR is a simulation on real geometry, labeled as such.
- The **deterministic policy floor is the only judge.** Never an LLM grading an LLM. The veto comes from `isDestructive`/`socHandler.ts:140`, not the Guardian LLM.
- The invariant counter **binds to real run data.** If it ever falls back to mock, it is labeled "mock." A fake 0 a judge probes loses the room.
- Cross-family numbers are **PLATFORM comparisons** (same gemma-4-31b, Cerebras WSE vs GPU). The **2,522/1,038 is Llama-4-Maverick WSE-vs-B200** (`bench.js:10`) — never conflated with gemma-4-31b in the same breath.
- Foundry numbers are the **real** ones: 40 scenarios / 120 raw samples / 17 overrides / 9 refuses / 869 tok/s measured. Not "4 refuses," not "~1,500 tok/s on this run."
- Regulatory citations pulled **verbatim** from the guardian-agent-foundations references, not paraphrased.
- DeepMind D/R sub-labels framed as "mapped to the detect/respond taxonomy" — we do not invent per-ID definitions we haven't re-verified.
- "Auditable / fail-closed soundness" — **never "formally verified."**
- **Drafts only** for X/Discord. Never auto-post.

**Top risks + mitigations:**

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Two-codebase split burns the window | **Resolved up front:** Origin `/soc` is the single hero surface; quorum used only as a pre-recorded loop-race clip. Cuts the cross-repo `verifier.mjs`/`hash.ts` imports — `hash.ts` is already in-repo at `apps/origin-web/src/passport/hash.ts`. |
| P0 ≈ 14h vs two hero builds | Ship ONE hero: the invariant card + fault-injection beat. The embodied AMR ships as a video splice from already-verified offline data, not a net-new live preset. |
| Live Cerebras key rate-limits during recording | Pre-record against the verified-live INC-4 veto + loop-race; labeled mock fallback is acceptable for a screen-recording. |
| Invariant counter wired to a hardcoded 0 | Bind to `socRun`/`socRace` responses; assert real data in the recording; never ship a static 0. |
| Name change breaks tests | Copy-only: hero header + X post + docs. No file/route/handler renames. 220/220 stays green. |
| Wrong compliance control ID | Verify every citation against the skill references before it goes on screen. |
| Posting-window scramble | Stage post + MP4 + reply bank in drafts the night before; posting is one tap. |
